titleStmt.title: Letter from James Patrick Farrell to Lord Wimborne, 27 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Patrick Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from James Patrick Farrell (1865-1921), MP for North Longford, to the desk of the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland Sir Ivor Churchill Guest, Lord Wimborne (1873-1939). James Patrick Farrell (1865-1921) was an MP in West Cavan after the 1895 by-election and then North Longford from 1900 until 1918 general elections. Farrell was also founder, owner and editor of the Longford Leader newspaper.Ivor Churchill Guest, 1st Viscount Wimborne (1873-1939) was appointed the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland in 1915. Guest worked closely alongside Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the administration of Ireland. Following the suppression of the Rising, Guest, under pressure, was forced to resign as Lord Lieutenant. A later inquiry into the Rising exonerated Guest and held Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) chiefly responsible for the events that occurred. Guest would continue to serve as Lord Lieutenant until 1918.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6258/11

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Longdord March, 27th 1916
To the Right Hon. Lord Wimbourne,
Lord Lieutenant of Ireland,
Dublin. My Dear Lord Wimbourne -: Pardon the liberty I take as
an old Parliamentary Colleague of Your Excellency in
the House of Commons, to recommend to your favourable
notice the application of Mr. Thomas W. Delany, Crown
Solicitor of the County of Longford, for the position
of Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland, rendered vacant
by the death of Sir Malachy Kelly. I am sure that
amongst t he profession in Ireland,there are many able
Solicitors well fitted to discharge the duties of
Chief Crown Solicitor, but I respectfully say that in
my opinion not one of them can equal either in tact
or ability, the gentleman whose name I have the honour
to submit to Your Excellency. Mr. Delany has a record
of public service here which few men of his class in
Ireland can sayshow. He has in addition since the War began,
whole-heartedly thrown himself into the work of
securing recruits for the Irish Regiments. He is Vice- letter incomplete
